message:
fix(net): prevent UnixDatagram::bind from deleting non-socket files

Replace the unconditional `let _ = std::fs::remove_file(path)` in
UnixDatagram::bind() with a call to the crate's
remove_stale_socket_file() helper (whose visibility was widened to
pub(crate) in a prior commit). The old code would silently delete ANY
file at the bind path — including regular files, directories, and
symlinks — which could cause data loss if the path happened to point
at a non-socket file.

The new behavior:
- If path is a Unix socket: remove it (stale from a previous run)
- If path is any other file type: return io::ErrorKind::AlreadyExists
- If path doesn't exist: proceed with bind (no-op removal)

Add regression test test_bind_refuses_non_socket_path: creates a
regular file with known contents, attempts UnixDatagram::bind on it,
verifies the error kind is AlreadyExists, and confirms the file
contents are unchanged (not deleted).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
